Pre Conditions: Dev server running (pnpm dev), modern browser open
Steps: 1. Open http://localhost:5173 in browser 2. Wait for app to fully load 3. Check browser console for errors 4. Verify file explorer sidebar is visible
Expected Result: App loads with file explorer sidebar, editor welcome screen, terminal, and status bar visible. No console errors. Toast notifications may appear for initialization.

Steps: 1. Look at the bottom status bar 2. Verify it shows line/column info and encoding
Expected Result: Status bar shows 'Ln 1, Col 1', 'UTF-8'. When a file is open, also shows file path and language indicator (e.g., 'ts').

Steps: 1. Open terminal panel 2. Wait for WebContainer boot 3. Check for 'Ready' status indicator
Expected Result: Terminal shows 'WebContainer VM Ready' message. Status indicator shows green 'Ready'. Command prompt '$' appears.

Test Case: Create a new file via toolbar
Steps: 1. Click 'New File' icon in explorer toolbar 2. Type 'test.ts' in the inline input 3. Press Enter
Expected Result: New file 'test.ts' appears in explorer with TypeScript icon. Toast shows 'Created test.ts'. File saved to IndexedDB.

Test Case: Create a new file via context menu
Steps: 1. Right-click in file explorer area 2. Select 'New File' from context menu 3. Type 'utils.js' and press Enter
Expected Result: New file 'utils.js' appears with JavaScript icon. Context menu closes after selection.

Steps: 1. Click 'New Folder' icon in explorer toolbar 2. Type 'src' and press Enter 3. Verify folder appears
Expected Result: New folder 'src' appears in explorer with folder icon. Folder is expandable/collapsible.

Steps: 1. Right-click on test.ts 2. Select 'Rename' 3. Clear and type 'renamed.ts' 4. Press Enter
Expected Result: File name updates to 'renamed.ts' in explorer. If file was open in editor, tab name updates. File icon updates if extension changed.

Pre Conditions: File exists in explorer, file may be open in editor
Steps: 1. Right-click on a file 2. Select 'Delete' 3. Confirm deletion if prompted
Expected Result: File disappears from explorer. If file was open in editor, tab closes. File removed from IndexedDB.

Pre Conditions: Folder with files exists in explorer
Steps: 1. Right-click on folder 2. Select 'Delete' 3. Confirm deletion
Expected Result: Folder and all contents removed recursively. Any open files from that folder close.

Steps: 1. Create files with various extensions 2. Observe file icons in explorer tree
Expected Result: Each file shows an appropriate language icon: TypeScript, React, JSON, Markdown, CSS, HTML, Python.

Steps: 1. Click into nested folders 2. Observe breadcrumb path bar 3. Click 'Go to root' button 4. Click 'Go up directory' button
Expected Result: Path bar shows current directory. Root button returns to '/'. Up button navigates one level up. Buttons disabled when at root.

Steps: 1. Click on a .ts file in the explorer 2. Observe the editor area 3. Verify syntax highlighting
Expected Result: File opens in a new tab with correct filename. Monaco editor loads with TypeScript syntax highlighting. Cursor positioned at line 1.

Steps: 1. Click file1.ts to open 2. Click file2.json to open 3. Click file3.md to open 4. Click between tabs
Expected Result: All three tabs exist. Clicking switches between files. Content is preserved per tab. Only one tab is active (highlighted) at a time.

Pre Conditions: File open with unsaved modifications (dot indicator on tab)
Steps: 1. Type some text in the editor 2. Observe modified dot (●) on tab 3. Press Ctrl+S (or Cmd+S on Mac) 4. Observe tab indicator
Expected Result: Modified dot (●) disappears from tab. Toast shows 'Saved: /filename'. File persisted to IndexedDB.

Steps: 1. Focus a file tab by clicking it 2. Press Ctrl+W
Expected Result: Active tab closes. Next available tab becomes active. If last tab closes, welcome screen appears.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+F 2. Type a search term that exists in the file 3. Observe highlights 4. Press Enter to cycle through matches
Expected Result: Monaco find widget appears at top of editor. Matching text highlighted in yellow. Navigation arrows cycle through matches. Match count shown.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+H 2. Type search term in 'Find' field 3. Type replacement in 'Replace' field 4. Click 'Replace All'
Expected Result: All occurrences replaced. Replace count shown in find widget. File shows modified dot on tab.

Steps: 1. Open a .ts file → TypeScript highlighting 2. Open a .json file → JSON highlighting 3. Open a .md file → Markdown highlighting 4. Open a .css file → CSS highlighting
Expected Result: Each file shows correct syntax colors: keywords, strings, comments, brackets colored appropriately per language.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+` (backtick) 2. Observe bottom panel
Expected Result: Terminal panel appears with 'Browser IDE Terminal' header, 'WebContainer VM Ready' message, and command prompt '$'.

Steps: 1. Type 'echo hello world' and press Enter 2. Type 'ls' and press Enter 3. Type 'pwd' and press Enter
Expected Result: echo outputs 'hello world'. ls shows file listing with icons. pwd shows current directory '/'. Prompt returns after each command.

Steps: 1. Type 'node -e \while(true){}\"' and press Enter 2. Wait 1 second 3. Press Ctrl+C"
Expected Result: Running process is interrupted. Prompt '$' returns. Terminal remains responsive for new commands.

Pre Conditions: Terminal open with several lines of output
Steps: 1. Run 3-4 commands to generate output 2. Press Ctrl+L
Expected Result: Terminal output cleared. Fresh prompt shown at top of terminal area. Command history still accessible.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+Shift+M (or click maximize button) 2. Observe terminal size 3. Press Ctrl+Shift+M again (or Escape)
Expected Result: Terminal expands to fill entire editor area. Button tooltip changes. Second press restores to original size. Editor area reappears.

Pre Conditions: Terminal open, package.json exists in project
Steps: 1. Type 'npm init -y' and press Enter 2. Type 'npm install lodash' 3. Observe installation
Expected Result: package.json created. npm install downloads package. node_modules appears in file explorer. No CORS errors in console.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+Shift+G 2. Observe bottom panel switches to Git
Expected Result: Git panel opens showing Changes/History/Branches/Stash tabs. If no repo exists, shows appropriate empty state message.

Steps: 1. Click 'Clone Repository' button (or '📥 Clone' in toolbar) 2. Enter a small public repo HTTPS URL 3. Click Clone 4. Wait for completion
Expected Result: Clone dialog shows progress. On completion, files appear in explorer. Git status indicator appears in status bar. Toast confirms success.

Pre Conditions: Repo cloned, file modified in editor
Steps: 1. Modify a file and save 2. Open Git panel (Ctrl+Shift+G) 3. See file in Changes list 4. Click '+' to stage 5. Type commit message 6. Press Ctrl+Enter
Expected Result: File moves from 'Changes' to 'Staged'. Commit succeeds. Toast shows success. Changes list empties. History tab shows new commit.

Pre Conditions: Git panel open, repo with history
Steps: 1. Click/press Ctrl+1 for Changes tab 2. Click/press Ctrl+2 for History tab 3. Click/press Ctrl+3 for Branches tab 4. Click/press Ctrl+4 for Stash tab
Expected Result: Each tab loads its view correctly. Changes shows file status. History shows commit log. Branches shows branch list. Stash shows stash entries.

Steps: 1. Open Git panel 2. Switch to History tab 3. Scroll through commits
Expected Result: Commit list shows: commit message, author name, timestamp. Commits in reverse chronological order. Click on commit may show details.

Steps: 1. Open Git panel → Branches tab 2. Click 'Create Branch' 3. Type 'feature/test' and press Enter 4. Verify branch appears 5. Switch back to main
Expected Result: New branch created and checked out. Status bar shows 'feature/test'. Switching back to main updates status bar. File states preserved.

Test Case: Open AI panel from tab bar
Steps: 1. Click the AI/Claude tab in bottom tab bar 2. Observe the panel content
Expected Result: AI chat panel opens with message input area at bottom, empty message history, model selector.

Steps: 1. Open Settings (gear icon) 2. Navigate to AI section 3. Enter a valid API key (e.g., Anthropic key starting with 'sk-ant-') 4. Select model 5. Close settings
Expected Result: API key saved. Key encrypted via Web Crypto API. Model selector reflects chosen model. No key visible in plain text after saving.

Pre Conditions: API key configured, AI panel open
Steps: 1. Type 'What is TypeScript?' in chat input 2. Press Ctrl+Enter (or Cmd+Enter)
Expected Result: User message appears in chat. Loading indicator shows. AI response streams in character by character. Response rendered with formatting.

Test Case: Open help panel from tab bar
Steps: 1. Click the 'Help' tab (📚) in bottom tab bar
Expected Result: Help panel opens with sidebar showing 4 categories: Getting Started, Quick Reference, Features, Advanced. Default section content loads.

Steps: 1. Click Getting Started → verify content 2. Click Quick Reference → Keyboard Shortcuts 3. Click Quick Reference → Command Palette 4. Click Features → File Explorer 5. Click Features → Editor 6. Click Features → Terminal 7. Click Features → Git Integration 8. Click Features → AI Assistant 9. Click Features → Project Management 10. Click Advanced → Offline Mode 11. Click Advanced → Troubleshooting
Expected Result: Each section displays rich content: headings, bullet lists, shortcut tables (with <kbd> styled keys), code blocks, and tip boxes. Content area scrolls for longer sections.

Steps: 1. Click search input 2. Type 'git' 3. Observe sidebar filters 4. Clear search 5. Type 'isomorphic' 6. Observe results 7. Type 'xyznonexistent'
Expected Result: 'git' → shows Git Integration and related sections. 'isomorphic' → shows Git (deep searchText match). Nonsense → all categories disappear, sidebar empty.

Steps: 1. Click the X (close) button in help panel header
Expected Result: Help panel closes. Previous panel (e.g., Terminal) may become visible or panel area shows empty state.

Steps: 1. Press Ctrl+Shift+P (or Cmd+Shift+P) 2. Type 'save' 3. Observe filtered command list 4. Press Enter on 'Save File' 5. Verify action executed
Expected Result: Command Palette overlay appears. Typing filters commands in real-time. Each command shows name, description, shortcut. Selecting executes the command. Palette closes.

Steps: 1. Open Command Palette 2. Type 'toggle' 3. Select 'Toggle Sidebar' → sidebar toggles 4. Reopen palette 5. Select 'Toggle Terminal' → terminal toggles
Expected Result: Each toggle command changes the corresponding UI element visibility. Commands execute immediately. Palette closes after each selection.

Steps: 1. Click Terminal tab → terminal content shows 2. Click Help tab → help content shows 3. Click back to Terminal
Expected Result: Each click switches panel content. Active tab highlighted with blue bottom border. Inactive tabs gray. Content persists between switches.

Steps: 1. Focus tab bar by clicking a tab 2. Press ArrowRight → next tab activates 3. Press ArrowLeft → previous tab activates 4. Press Home → first tab 5. Press End → last tab
Expected Result: Arrow keys move one tab at a time. Home/End jump to first/last. Does not wrap around edges. Focus follows active tab.

Pre Conditions: App at mobile width (375px), touch device or mobile emulation
Steps: 1. Touch and swipe left on tab bar (>50px) 2. Touch and swipe right on tab bar (>50px) 3. Try a short swipe (<50px) 4. Try a vertical swipe
Expected Result: Left swipe → next tab. Right swipe → previous tab. Short swipe → no change (below 50px threshold). Vertical swipe → no change (filtered). Dots indicator updates.

Steps: 1. Click gear icon (⚙️) in top toolbar 2. Or: open Command Palette → type 'settings'
Expected Result: Settings dialog opens as modal overlay. Shows sections: Editor, Git, AI, Appearance, Terminal, Monitoring. Each section has relevant fields.

Test Case: Change editor theme and font size
Pre Conditions: Settings dialog open, file open in editor
Steps: 1. Change theme from 'vs-dark' to another option 2. Change font size from 14 to 18 3. Close settings dialog 4. Observe editor
Expected Result: Editor reflects new theme colors. Text renders at font size 18. Settings persisted to IndexedDB (survive page reload).

Steps: 1. Navigate to Git section 2. Enter username: 'testuser' 3. Enter email: 'test@example.com' 4. Close settings
Expected Result: Git settings saved. Future commits use this author info. Settings persist across sessions.

Pre Conditions: App loaded with files in project
Steps: 1. Open Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P) 2. Select 'Export Project' 3. Enter project name 4. Click Export
Expected Result: Project downloads as a file. Contains all project files, settings, and metadata. Toast confirms export success.

Pre Conditions: Exported project file available on disk
Steps: 1. Open Command Palette 2. Select 'Import Project' 3. Select the exported file from file picker 4. Wait for import
Expected Result: Project files restored in explorer. Settings restored. Open files and editor state may reset. Toast confirms import success.

Steps: 1. Resize browser window to 375px width 2. Observe sidebar behavior 3. Observe top toolbar 4. Observe bottom tab bar 5. Observe file explorer
Expected Result: Sidebar becomes overlay modal with 'Files' header and close button. Top toolbar shows condensed icons. Bottom tabs show short labels ('Term', 'Help') with icons. Dots indicator visible between tabs and content.

Steps: 1. Resize browser to 768px width 2. Observe layout
Expected Result: Sidebar may be visible or collapsible. Bottom tabs show full labels. Editor takes remaining horizontal space. No horizontal overflow.

Steps: 1. Resize browser to 1280px width 2. Observe layout
Expected Result: Full layout: sidebar (w-64) + resizable editor + bottom panel. All elements visible simultaneously. No scrollbar issues. Panel resizers functional.

Pre Conditions: App loaded with open files and terminal history
Steps: 1. Open a file 2. Type in terminal 3. Press F5 or Ctrl+R to reload 4. Wait for app to reinitialize
Expected Result: App reloads. Previously open files may reopen (if persisted). Terminal reinitializes. Settings preserved. No crash or error.

Test Case: No console errors on clean start
Pre Conditions: Fresh browser profile or cleared IndexedDB
Steps: 1. Clear IndexedDB for localhost:5173 2. Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) 3. Check console for errors
Expected Result: Zero console errors. Only info/debug logs from logger utility. No unhandled promise rejections.

Test Case: File operations work without Git repo
Steps: 1. Create files and folders 2. Edit and save files 3. Open terminal and run commands
Expected Result: All file operations work normally. Git panel shows empty state. No errors from missing .git directory.

Test Case: Create file with special characters in name
Steps: 1. Create new file 2. Type name with spaces: 'my file.ts' 3. Try name with unicode: '日本語.md'
Expected Result: File created with exact name entered. File icon correct. File opens in editor without issues.

Steps: 1. Create or import a file >1MB 2. Click to open in editor
Expected Result: File opens (may take a moment). Editor may show performance warning. Syntax highlighting may be limited for very large files.

Test Case: Multiple browser tabs open same IDE
Steps: 1. Open http://localhost:5173 in a second tab 2. Create a file in tab 1 3. Refresh tab 2
Expected Result: Both tabs function independently. IndexedDB changes from tab 1 visible in tab 2 after refresh. No data corruption.

Steps: 1. Load app normally 2. Disconnect network (DevTools → Network → Offline) 3. Try file operations 4. Try terminal commands
Expected Result: File operations work (IndexedDB is local). Terminal commands that don't need network work. AI Assistant shows connection error. Git push/pull fail gracefully with error message.

Pre Conditions: Dev server running, browser cache cleared
Steps: 1. Hard refresh the page (Ctrl+Shift+R) 2. Measure time from navigation start to interactive 3. Check Web Vitals in console
Expected Result: App is fully interactive within 3 seconds. TTFB < 100ms (local). LCP < 2500ms. No layout shifts (CLS near 0).

Test Case: No memory leaks on repeated file open/close
Steps: 1. Open and close 20+ files in sequence 2. Monitor browser memory usage (DevTools → Performance → Memory) 3. Compare start and end memory
Expected Result: Memory usage does not continuously grow. May increase slightly but stabilizes. No 'out of memory' errors.

Steps: 1. Type rapidly in terminal for 10 seconds 2. Paste a long command (100+ chars) 3. Run a command that produces large output (e.g., 'node -e \for(let i=0;i<1000;i++)console.log(i)\"')"
Expected Result: Terminal keeps up with rapid typing. Long commands display without breaking layout. Large output renders without freezing UI.
